title: Upgrade building tools
body:
This is still work in progress, opening the PR so we can track the progress.

Linked issues #2168 #1866 

## Summary

The build mechanism has not been updated with the v2. Now that version is live, we will upgrade the building tools in order to have the most productive development environment 🚀

I did an experiment by replacing `node-sass` with `postcss` but it's too much overhead, we will sync about this when we want to provide a single shared theme in between instantsearch libraries.

I also did an experiment with rollup instead of webpack, but there's no real advantages of doing this. It adds more dependancies and now webpack3 has reached the state of rollup, maybe goes further with [scope hoisting](https://medium.com/webpack/brief-introduction-to-scope-hoisting-in-webpack-8435084c171f) 🌟

* [x] Upgrade dev & staging build to use webpack v3
* [x] `$ yarn dev` and `$ yarn staging` shares now the same `dev/webpack.config.js`
* [x] Remove the `.babel.js` from webpack config to load faster since we use now nodejs7+ we can use ES6 features without transpiling through babel
* [x] Upgrade docgen to use webpack3
* [x] Upgrade final build to use `babel` CLI for ES6 modules
* [x] Use `babel-preset-env` with `modules: false` (Tree shaking)
* [x] Upgrade final build to use webpack3 for browsers builds
* [x] Simplify and clean every `scripts/*.sh` (since we use almost webpack to do all the stuff, we can have one liner commands into `package.json` scripts)
* [x] Document usage with rollup/wepback2+ for tree shaking

More detailed changes for the dev env:

* use of webpack [style-loader](https://github.com/webpack-contrib/style-loader) + [sass-loader](https://github.com/webpack-contrib/sass-loader) instead of building and watching with `node-sass` CLI
     -> This introduce a speed gain as well of having the "hot-reload" of styles
* use of [html-webpack-plugin](https://github.com/jantimon/html-webpack-plugin) to generate an `index.html` with hashed assets
     -> Cache buster when deploying to staging
* use of [autodll-webpack-plugin](https://github.com/asfktz/autodll-webpack-plugin) which build vendors seperatly
     -> Speed up re-build on dev (big external libraries not re-builded)
* use of [happypack](https://github.com/amireh/happypack) which allow to transform multiple files in parallel
      -> Speed up initial build and re-build

## Results

**Staging build** is 50% faster (removed the minification on new branch to have a fair comparison):

```
BEFORE:
➔ time (node-sass -o ./dev/ ./src/css --output-style expanded && webpack --config dev/webpack.staging.config.babel.js --hot --inline --no-info)

~12s

AFTER:
➔ time NODE_ENV=production webpack --config dev/webpack.config.js

~6s
```

**Production build** stays quite the same, no build size changes but everything is bundled by webpack.

milestone.description:
The goal of this milestone is to solidify the new 2.0 branch:
 - Update the test stack
 - Update the packaging stack
 - Refactor to improve robustness
